summary: An original cobalt-on-warm-white decorative system inspired by blue-and-white porcelain, using disciplined negative space, brush variation, framed motifs, and ceramic ornament rather than a generic blue floral print.
visual_vocabulary: cobalt underglaze brushwork with varied line weight
framed medallions and continuous border bands
layered foreground motif and restrained supporting ornament
material_cues: slight cobalt pigment pooling at selected brush turns
clean matte albedo with no baked highlights
optional sparse glaze crackle reserved for a later Gunsmith material layer
composition_principles: use one dominant motif family and subordinate borders
vary scale deliberately instead of filling every surface uniformly
keep motifs readable after projection onto a narrow weapon silhouette
avoid: baked lighting, reflections, ambient occlusion, text, logos, watermarks

route_a.objective: Create an elegant seamless all-over porcelain pattern that remains complete under arbitrary tiling and UV placement.
composition_rules: build a square edge-to-edge repeat with no single irreplaceable focal scene
balance medium floral or landscape motifs with quiet white intervals

route_b.objective: Compose a weapon-level porcelain artwork in continuous weapon space, then bake it into the fragmented UV atlas.
composition_rules: place a primary framed scene on the receiver
continue border rhythm across stock, receiver, and handguard
treat the magazine as a secondary porcelain panel
reduce detail toward the barrel and muzzle

component_roles.stock: broad cloud-scroll transition with generous white ground
component_roles.receiver: primary framed narrative or floral medallion
component_roles.magazine: secondary vase-panel composition or lotus vignette
component_roles.pistol_grip: dark cobalt grounding band with sparse ornament
component_roles.handguard: continuous ruyi or wave border connecting the receiver to the front
component_roles.barrel_muzzle: mostly quiet porcelain white or deep cobalt with no focal detail

candidate_directions.title: Peony Medallion
candidate_directions.concept: A generous peony medallion supported by cloud scrolls and a restrained ruyi border.
candidate_directions.route_a_emphasis: interlocking half-medallions form a seamless repeat without a fixed centre
candidate_directions.route_b_emphasis: one complete peony medallion becomes the receiver hero and the borders create longitudinal rhythm

candidate_directions.title: Landscape Scroll
candidate_directions.concept: An airy mountain-and-water scroll with misty negative space and a wave-band base.
candidate_directions.route_a_emphasis: small landscape islands alternate across a repeating field
candidate_directions.route_b_emphasis: the weapon reads as one horizontal handscroll from stock to handguard

candidate_directions.title: Lotus Pond
candidate_directions.concept: Lotus blossoms and leaves use sweeping stems to link broad porcelain-white spaces.
candidate_directions.route_a_emphasis: curved stems cross tile edges and reconnect into an organic repeat
candidate_directions.route_b_emphasis: a large lotus anchors the receiver while stems guide the eye across components

candidate_directions.title: Ruyi Brocade
candidate_directions.concept: A more graphic interpretation combining ruyi heads, framed geometry, and sparse floral accents.
candidate_directions.route_a_emphasis: bold medium-scale geometry makes the tile highly readable
candidate_directions.route_b_emphasis: geometric bands follow component proportions while the receiver retains a single floral seal

evaluation_criteria: originality rather than imitation of a single artifact
absence of baked lighting and forbidden content
reference_policy: Use curated museum/public-domain or clearly licensed references only as a vocabulary set; record provenance and never reproduce one identifiable artifact or maker's pattern.
